issues.code:
1 import json
2 import pickle
3 from base64 import b64decode, b64encode
issues.description: Consider possible security implications associated with pickle module.

issues.code:
9     try:
10         metadata = pickle.loads(b64decode(request["metadata_blob"]))
11     except Exception as e:
issues.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.

issues.code:
17             cursor = db.cursor()
18             cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM invoice_lines WHERE invoice_id=? AND {request['filter_clause']}", (request["invoice_id"],))
19             lines = [dict(line) for line in cursor]
issues.description: Possible SQL injection vector through string-based query construction.
Pickle deserialization of untrusted data can lead to code execution; avoid pickle for untrusted inputs.

issues.code:
9     try:
10         metadata = pickle.loads(b64decode(request["metadata_blob"]))
11     except Exception as e:
issues.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.
issues.judge_reason: The code uses pickle.loads on user-supplied base64-decoded data with no restrictions. Pickle deserialization can execute arbitrary code. No allowlist or safe alternative is used.

issues.code:
9     try:
10         metadata = pickle.loads(b64decode(request["metadata_blob"]))
11     except Exception as e:
issues.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.
issues.judge_reason: The code uses pickle.loads on user-supplied base64-decoded data with no restrictions. Pickle deserialization can execute arbitrary code. No allowlist or safe alternative is used.

issues.code:
17             cursor = db.cursor()
18             cursor.execute(f"SELECT * FROM invoice_lines WHERE invoice_id=? AND {request['filter_clause']}", (request["invoice_id"],))
19             lines = [dict(line) for line in cursor]
issues.description: Possible SQL injection vector through string-based query construction.
issues.judge_reason: The filter_clause is directly interpolated into the SQL query string via f-string. Although the invoice_id is parameterized, the filter_clause is not, allowing SQL injection. There is no validation or sanitization of filter_clause.

duplicate_findings.code:
1 import json
2 import pickle
3 from base64 import b64decode, b64encode
duplicate_findings.description: Consider possible security implications associated with pickle module.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: Importing pickle itself is flagged as a risk because the module is known to be unsafe for untrusted data. This is the same root cause as B301; B301 is more specific (the actual deserialization call). Marking as duplicate of B301#line10.
